business.headline: 8th Pay Commission update: Has the last date been extended amid employee demands?The Commission is holding a three-day consultation session from April 28 to April 30. Rajiv Bajaj to step down from Bajaj Finance board at end of termRajiv Bajaj's tenure as a non-executive director will conclude at the close of the company's upcoming annual general meeting, in line with regulatory requirements.

Odisha bank case explained: Rules for withdrawing money after deathOnce an account holder dies, the account is no longer treated as a normal account. Any access to funds becomes a claim settlement process, not a simple withdrawal.

A wealth start-up founder breaks down the Rs 40-crore retirement ruleDezerv co-founder Sandeep Jethwani said on a podcast that a metro retiree may need Rs 40 crore by 60. His calculation hinges on urban inflation, longer lifespans and the gap between nominal returns and real gains.

Health claim denied for late filing? Here's what you need to knowMany policyholders have seen their claims rejected just for missing deadlines, even when the expenses were genuine. However, a recent court ruling brings much-needed relief. Here are the details.

Gains across auto, banking, IT, energy and consumption stocks pointed to a broad-based recovery in sentiment.

This new form replaces 15G, 15H as PF rules get simpler. What it means for youFor years, EPF subscribers have been used to filing Forms 15G and 15H. That process has now changed. A single new form has replaced both, making PF rules simpler, but what does it mean for you?

Quant funds or traditional mutual funds: What works better for Indians today?Quant brings scalability, discipline, and the power of data. Traditional brings context, adaptability, and judgement. As markets evolve and technology gets more deeply embedded in how money is managed, the investors who do well will not be the ones who pick sides.

Can India handle oil over $100? What it means for growth, inflation, marketsThe West Asia conflict has pushed up crude prices and exposed India's dependence on energy imports. How long the war lasts will decide the hit to growth, inflation, the rupee and markets.

Shefali Shah's Dance Day video on Sapne Mein Milti Hai is pure 90s nostalgia Shefali Shah shared a candid video of herself dancing to the song Sapne Mein Milti Hai on International Dance Day. The playful post revived memories of the 1998 film Satya and the iconic song.

Lara Dutta recalls Deepika filming Housefull with malaria: She was totally drained Lara Dutta shared that Deepika Padukone was diagnosed with malaria during the shoot of Housefull but continued filming despite battling high fever and exhaustion.

Samantha's birthday with fans goes viral: Themed cake, special photo frame, gifts Samantha celebrated her 39th birthday with fans at a special meet-and-greet. A viral video from the celebration gave a glimpse of the fun, warmth, and heartfelt moments she shared with her admirers.

Revisiting Kyunki's darkest chapter: Akashdeep Saigal, marital rape, Tulsi's horror Kyunki Saas Bhi Kabhi Bahu Thi 2 revisits its darkest past as Akashdeep Saigal returns in a new avatar, stirring old memories and emotions. Tumbbad 2 to expand franchise's dark folklore world, release date locked in 2027 Actor and co-producer Sohum Shah has announced the release date of his upcoming film, Tumbbad 2. The film is a sequel to the 2018 cult horror fantasy, Tumbbad, with actor Nawazuddin Siddiqui joining the cast.

The account underscored how training, screen tests and family reactions shaped the biopic's most scrutinised decision.

Bihar distributors queue up for Allu Arjun's Raaka rights; here's what we know Actor Allu Arjun's Raaka is drawing massive interest from distributors in Bihar for exclusive state rights. The buzz reflects his strong fan base in the Hindi belt after the success of Pushpa: The Rise and Pushpa 2: The Rule.

Allu Arjun-Lokesh Kanagaraj film's script work begins: Writer Rathna Kumar Writer-director Rathna Kumar said script work has begun on AA23, starring Allu Arjun and directed by Lokesh Kanagaraj. He also addressed the Jana Nayagan leak and said Leo 2 is not on the cards now.

Male actors didn't stop after cut: Annu Kapoor on Bollywood intimate scene shoots Annu Kapoor recently spoke about actors losing control during intimate scenes, and recalled one case where a co-actor did not stop even after the director said "cut".

general news.headline: Poll of polls: DMK in TN, Congress return in Kerala, NDA in Assam, Bengal suspenseA poll of exit polls have predicted a big win for the BJP in Assam and gave the party an edge ahead of the ruling TMC in West Bengal. The polls also suggested that the DMK is likely to return to power in Tamil Nadu, while the Congress-led UDF may make a comeback in Kerala after 10 years.

Vijay the next MGR: Axis My India exit poll predicts stunner in Tamil NaduA political debut shakes Tamil Nadu's two-party system as Axis My India exit poll hints at a generational shift. Can Vijay's TVK convert youth momentum into power, or will established players hold ground on counting day?

Exit polls favour BJP, but Mamata's appeal and Bengali identity may tighten raceEven as several exit polls have given the BJP an edge in West Bengal, ground sentiment suggests that Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's personal appeal and Bengali identity factor could keep the contest much tighter than projections indicate.

Iranians are choking like stuffed pig: Trump says blockade stays until nuclear dealDonald Trump rejected Iran's proposal and said US will keep Strait of Hormuz blockade in place. He believes Iran's energy infrastructure is nearing a breaking point, with its oil storage and pipelines getting close to exploding.

1 litre of ethanol needs 10,000 litres water: Hidden cost of India's fuel pushIndia's total ethanol production capacity stands at 1,822 crore litres, with a disproportionate share concentrated in already water-stressed states like Delhi and Maharashtra.

Iran war not quagmire, shame on you: Pete Hegseth confronts Democrats in CongressUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth faced heated questioning in Congress over the Iran war's cost and strategy. The Pentagon's $1.45 trillion budget request, which is a roughly 40 per cent increase over this year's budget, is the subject of the hearing.

12 workers killed, 13 injured as pickup truck overturns in Madhya PradeshEyewitnesses reported a scene of chaos and panic as the vehicle, which was reportedly overloaded beyond capacity, flipped multiple times.

Live: No deal unless Iran agrees to abandon nuclear weapons, Trump saysUS-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has rejected Iran's proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade, choosing to tighten pressure instead of easing it. He argued the blockade is more effective than military strikes, signalling that Washington is relying on economic strain rather than immediate escalation to force Tehran's hand. That pressure is already showing. Iran's currency has slipped to a record low of 1,810,000 rials to the dollar, according to state-linked media, highlighting the growing toll on its economy. Behind the scenes, the US appears to be settling in for a longer standoff. Trump and his aides have held talks with energy executives on how to keep the blockade in place for months while trying to shield American consumers from rising costs. The political heat is rising at home as well. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the war during a congressional hearing, brushing aside criticism and calling opponents "the biggest adversary" as he faced lawmakers for the first time since the conflict began. Diplomacy, meanwhile, remains stuck. Pakistan is expected to receive a revised Iranian proposal, but Trump has shown little patience, warning Tehran to "better get smart soon." He also spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin, who offered to help address Iran's uranium stockpiles, adding another layer to the ongoing negotiations. At the same time, Israel has launched fresh strikes in southern Lebanon, killing emergency workers and soldiers. Israeli forces have made it clear there is "no ceasefire" in their campaign against Hezbollah, despite ongoing efforts for direct talks. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.
Hailstorm turns parts of Bengaluru white, brings sudden relief from heatThe sudden weather event comes amid a prolonged spell of above-normal temperatures across Karnataka.

BJP edge in Bengal, say 4 exit polls, two predict Mamata Banerjee winFifteen years is a long time in holding power in a state. The 2026 election was billed by the BJP as a make-or-break battle. BJP leaders confident of victory, Opposition urges caution ahead of resultsExit Poll Results Highlights: Exit polls for the 2026 Assembly elections indicate a mixed but decisive mandate across key states, with regional dynamics shaping outcomes. In Assam, the NDA is projected to sweep comfortably, while Kerala appears set for a change in power with the UDF gaining a clear edge over the LDF. In Tamil Nadu, the DMK+ alliance is likely to retain power with a solid majority, as TVK+ emerges as a notable third force. West Bengal remains a close contest, though exit polls give the BJP a slight advantage. While these projections offer early signals, the final verdict will be known on counting day.

Good news for India: UAE can now send more oil via Fujairah, bypassing HormuzThe exit from the OPEC grouping has freed the UAE from Saudi-decided production caps. It can raise petroleum output and route more crude to India, using the Habshan-Fujairah oil pipeline, bypassing the Strait of Hormuz. The weakening of the oil cartel will strengthen India's energy security.

Why Modi's silence during elections screamsAs West Bengal voted, Narendra Modi's football outing in Sikkim and the Ganga Expressway launch in Uttar Pradesh drew national attention. Then dies by suicideAfter their separation, Nagalakshmi built an independent livelihood by launching Sree Sai Silks, a saree business, in Nanganallur, and expanded it through Instagram promotions. The business grew enough for her to open a larger store in Chennai.

DMK to retain Tamil Nadu, say pollsters; Vijay's TVK a big disruptor, one predictsThe DMK-led alliance is set to retain power in Tamil Nadu by a comfortable margin, according to most exit polls released on Wednesday, shrugging off the electoral challenge from the AIADMK-led alliance and actor-turned-politician Vijay's Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam (TVK). Rohit Sharma misses 4th straight IPL 2026 match: What's gone wrong hitman? IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma missed his fourth straight IPL 2026 match as Mumbai Indians faced Sunrisers Hyderabad. Hardik Pandya said the opener needs a couple more games, extending uncertainty around MI's batting.

Golfer Vijay Kumar, former India Open champion, dies at 57 Veteran Indian golfer Vijay Kumar died of a heart attack in Lucknow at 57. The 2002 Indian Open winner and four-time Order of Merit champion leaves behind a lasting legacy in Indian golf.
